user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard met. Requirement: The claimed object and relevant part should be visible clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The rear bumper of the car is clearly visible in img_1, where a medium-severity dent is present on its surface, consistent with the user's claim. No contradictions were found between the reported issue and the observed condition. Based on the visual evidence, the claim is supported.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. evidence_standard_met_reason: Evidence standard met. Requirement: The claimed object and relevant part should be visible clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The claim of a scratch on the front bumper is contradicted by visual evidence in img_1, which clearly shows a broken part on the front bumper rather than a scratch. The aggregated assessment reclassifies the issue as a broken_part with medium severity, warranting a contradicted status due to the mismatch between the claimed and observed condition.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. claim_status_justification: The submitted image (img_2) is too blurry and/or cropped to allow reliable evaluation of the claimed dent on the car door, so the evidence standard is not met. While the aggregation suggests a medium-severity dent on the door, this conclusion cannot be visually verified from the available evidence. As a result, there is not enough information to approve the claim.
